questions.question: What is the fastest land animal?
questions.explanation: The cheetah can reach speeds of up to 112 km/h.

questions.question: Which animal has the longest lifespan?
questions.explanation: Greenland sharks can live over 400 years.

questions.question: How many hearts does an octopus have?
questions.explanation: An octopus has 3 hearts: two pump blood to the gills, one to the body.

questions.question: What is the largest animal ever to have lived on Earth?
questions.explanation: The blue whale can reach 30 meters and weigh 180 tonnes.

questions.question: Which bird can fly backwards?
questions.explanation: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards.

questions.question: What is a group of flamingos called?
questions.explanation: A group of flamingos is called a flamboyance.

questions.question: Which mammal can truly fly?
questions.explanation: Bats are the only mammals capable of true sustained flight.

questions.question: How long is an elephant's pregnancy?
questions.explanation: Elephants have the longest pregnancy of any land animal at about 22 months.

questions.question: Which animal has the strongest bite force?
questions.explanation: The saltwater crocodile has the strongest measured bite at ~3,700 PSI.

questions.question: What color is a polar bear's skin?
questions.explanation: Polar bears have black skin under their translucent, hollow fur.

questions.question: Which insect is responsible for the most human deaths?
questions.explanation: Mosquitoes kill over 700,000 people annually by spreading malaria and other diseases.

questions.question: How many legs does a spider have?
questions.explanation: All spiders have 8 legs (they are arachnids, not insects).

questions.question: Which animal sleeps the most per day?
questions.explanation: Koalas sleep up to 22 hours a day due to their low-energy eucalyptus diet.

questions.question: What is the only continent without native reptile species?
questions.explanation: Antarctica is too cold for reptiles, which are cold-blooded.

questions.question: Which fish is the fastest in the ocean?
questions.explanation: The sailfish can reach speeds of up to 110 km/h.
